inventory_notes.detail: temperature_2m, cloud_cover, wind_speed_10m, shortwave_radiation, total_load, generation_forecast
old/weather_2015_2025.csv uses a different timestamp format and different scaling such as cloud_cover 34 instead of 0.236.

price_single_variable_interpretations.plain_language: As wind_speed_10m rises, fitted same-time price falls on average.

leakage_risks.feature: calendar features (hour, day_of_week, month, is_weekend, cyclical hour)
leakage_risks.reason: Calendar information is known at forecast time.

leakage_risks.feature: lagged prices created only from historical values
leakage_risks.reason: Past prices are available at forecast time.

leakage_risks.feature: rolling features computed only from past windows
leakage_risks.reason: Past-only windows do not leak future target information.
Observed future weather is not available for a 48-hour-ahead forecast unless forecast data is used.
The name suggests a forecast, but the dataset does not include forecast issuance timestamps or vintages.
Observed future load would leak; forecasted load could be safe if only the ex-ante forecast is used.

leakage_risks.feature: future observed production or weather values aligned directly with the target horizon
leakage_risks.reason: These values would not be known when issuing the forecast.

leakage_risks.feature: centered rolling windows or features derived from future prices
leakage_risks.reason: They use future observations from the prediction period.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: Long-run target history with volatility shifts and extreme spikes.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Establishes that the target is non-stationary and heavy-tailed.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: The switch from hourly to quarter-hour price resolution at the start of 2025.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Explains why the current dataset cannot yet support a fully aligned 15-minute exogenous panel.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: When exogenous missing blocks occur over time.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Supports explicit imputation or exclusion decisions instead of silent row dropping.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: Initial structure among price, weather, system, and calendar variables.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Quickly highlights candidate features and redundant relationships.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: How exogenous-price correlation changes across historical lags.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Guides later lag-feature engineering without overclaiming forecasting value.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: How strongly each weather variable explains price versus total load in pairwise OLS screening.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Directly answers whether weather is more linearly tied to demand than to price.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: Which two-variable price regressions explain the most variance among all exogenous feature pairs.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Makes pair combinations like `cloud_cover + wind_speed_10m` comparable instead of anecdotal.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: Fit quality and residual behavior for the strongest exploratory linear model.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Shows both the usable signal and the remaining modeling gap.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: Relative influence of the multi-feature predictors on a comparable scale.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Supports feature selection and interpretation discussions.

recommended_figures.what_it_shows: Flagged price spikes and unusual low-price periods.
recommended_figures.why_it_matters: Highlights where robust forecasting methods and careful evaluation will matter most.
